title: betula × aurata var. rhombifolia
intro: Betula × aurata var. rhombifolia, also known as the hybrid white birch, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent qualities, which can help to reduce swelling, prevent infection, and tighten tissues. The health benefits of Betula × aurata var. rhombifolia include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and providing relief from digestive problems. The leaves and bark of this plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use.
